title: Pilosella corymbuloides
intro: Pilosella corymbuloides, also known as the Rough Hawk's Beard, is a perennial plant species within the dandelion family (Asteraceae). Traditionally, Pilosella corymbuloides has been used in folk medicine to treat various health issues, including digestive problems and inflammation, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of this plant include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Pilosella corymbuloides is often prepared as a tea or tincture, and it is also used in capsule or tablet form to support digestive health and alleviate symptoms of irritable bowel syndrome.
taxonomy: Pilosella corymbuloides is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. It belongs to the genus Pilosella, which is a group of plants commonly known as false dandelions. The genus Pilosella is further divided into several sections and series, with Pilosella corymbuloides being part of the section Corymbuloides. Pilosella corymbuloides is native to mountainous regions in Central and Eastern Europe. This plant is often found growing in rocky or alpine areas.
